raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want A) surface-level copyediting for clarity, concision, and tone only, or B) deeper research-writing improvements as well (reframing the contribution, checking argument consistency, assessing novelty and evidence alignment, proposing section-level reorganization, and citation adjustments)? Please reply A or B.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The request to 'Improve this draft' most directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should highlight where claims may need hedging or uncertainty to avoid overstating results.",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid amplifying claims (e.g., 'state-of-the-art') beyond what the provided evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "We will confine changes to improving the given text without adding new experiments or external claims.",
    "RX.4": "We will avoid edits that could mislead about performance, baselines, or compute costs, preventing downstream misinformation.",
    "RX.5": "We will preserve and clearly reference reported numbers and baselines so that edits remain traceable to the original text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want A) surface-level copyediting for clarity, concision, and tone only, or B) deeper research-writing improvements as well (reframing the contribution, checking argument consistency, assessing novelty and evidence alignment, proposing section-level reorganization, and citation adjustments)? Please reply A or B.

rationale.R1.6: The request to 'Improve this draft' most directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should highlight where claims may need hedging or uncertainty to avoid overstating results.
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid amplifying claims (e.g., 'state-of-the-art') beyond what the provided evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: We will confine changes to improving the given text without adding new experiments or external claims.
rationale.RX.4: We will avoid edits that could mislead about performance, baselines, or compute costs, preventing downstream misinformation.
rationale.RX.5: We will preserve and clearly reference reported numbers and baselines so that edits remain traceable to the original text.
